data.title: ArcelorMittal Q4 Adjusted Earnings Fall, Sales Rise
data.description:
ArcelorMittal reported Q4 adjusted earnings Thursday of $0.52 per share, down from $1.18 a year earlier.

A single analyst polled by FactSet expected $0.59.

Sales for the quarter ended Dec. 31 were $14.71 billion, up from $14.55 billion a year earlier.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $15.03 billion.

data.title: New China Life Insurance: He Xingda Resigned as Nonexecutive Director
Zoujaj Starts Commercial Production of New Glass Container Line
data.description:
The National Co. for Glass Industries (SASE:2150), d/b/a Zoujaj, began commercial production of its new glass container line.

The sixth production line boosts the Saudi Arabian glass container manufacturer's annual production capacity by 25,000 tons, according to a Thursday release.

data.title: Italian Construction Sector Starts 2025 with Modest Growth
data.description: The HCOB Italy Construction PMI edged down slightly to 50.9 in January 2025 from 51.2 in December 2024, marking the second consecutive month of expansion in the sector.

data.title: BioVersys Concludes CHF80 Million Swiss IPO
data.description:
BioVersys completed its initial public offering on the SIX Swiss Exchange, securing 80 million francs in gross proceeds, according to a Thursday release.

The biopharmaceutical company issued 2,083,333 new shares at 36 francs apiece, implying a market capitalization of 212.9 million francs.

The majority of the IPO proceeds will be used to further advance the group's lead asset BV100, while a portion is allotted for the clinical development of its second lead asset alpibectir with GSK . The remaining amount is intended for its preclinical pipeline.

BioVersys shares will commence trading on Friday under the ticker symbol 'BIOV'.

data.title: European Stocks Gain for 3rd Session, Earnings Eyed
data.description:
The STOXX 50 gained 0.4% and the STOXX 600 added 0.5% on Thursday, marking a third consecutive session of gains, boosted by strong corporate results.

Shares of AstraZeneca gained almost 4% after the company's earnings and revenue topped forecasts and the pharmaceutical giant said it expects further sales.

Societe Generale jumped about 7% after reporting a rise in Q4 revenue and saying it is targeting revenue growth and higher profitability for the year.

Maersk soared more than 6% after the company started a $2 billion share buyback program.

On the other hand, Volvo sank more than 7% after warning of a challenging 2025 and Kering fell 2.8% after announcing that Gucci creative director Sabato de Sarno is leaving after only two years.

Meanwhile, traders continue to monitor developments in the trade war.

data.title: Market Chatter: South Korea to Monitor China's Strategic Mineral Export Controls Amid US Trade Dispute
data.description:
South Korea's Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy plans to closely monitor and respond to the potential impacts of China's export restrictions on key strategic minerals, Yonhap News reported Wednesday.

The move comes after Beijing imposed at least a 10% tariff on American imports in retaliation for US tariffs, the report said.

China also unveiled export controls on five critical metals — tungsten, indium, bismuth, tellurium, and molybdenum — essential for semiconductors and display manufacturing, it said.

South Korea currently holds tungsten reserves for six months and molybdenum for three months. China supplies over 85% of South Korea's tungsten and more than 90 percent of its molybdenum, the report said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: AstraZeneca tops FTSE 100 on robust Q4 results and 2025 forecast
data.description:
** Shares of AstraZeneca up as much as 4.6% to 11,636p; hitting their highest since October 2024

** Co top pct gainer on FTSE 100 CURRENCYCOM:UK100 index

** Drugmaker logs Q4 core EPS and revenue just above company-compiled analysts' estimate

** Reports Q4 total revenue at $14.9 billion vs estimate of $14.2 billion

** Q4 core EPS of $2.09 above estimate of $2.07

** Co expects 2025 revenue to increase by a high single-digit percentage, with core earnings to grow in low double-digit percentage

** Analysts expect 2025 sales growth of 6.5% and profit increase of 12.6%, according to data compiled by LSEG

** "Astra looks well on the way to achieving its $80bn revenue target by 2030, but there may be some disappointment that this bar hasn't been raised" - Hargreaves Lansdown analyst

** Stock up about 10% over last 12 months

data.title: BioStock: Double celebration for Lipum
data.description:
Chordate Medical

Biotech company Lipum has two reasons to celebrate in 2025: not only its 15th anniversary but also the 80th birthday of its co-founder, Professor Olle Hernell. In an interview with BioStock, Hernell reflects on Lipum’s journey – from its academic research origins to becoming a publicly traded company developing a novel biologic drug.

Read the full article at biostock.se:

https://www.biostock.se/en/2025/02/double-celebration-for-lipum/

This is a press release from BioStock - Connecting Innovation & Capital. https://www.biostock.se

https://news.cision.com/chordate-medical/r/biostock-double-celebration-for-lipum%2Cc4101658

data.title: China, Hong Kong stocks rise as DeepSeek AI optimism lifts tech sector
data.description:
China and Hong Kong stocks advanced on Thursday, driven by the tech sector, as investors continued to bet on domestic artificial intelligence firms following Chinese AI start-up DeepSeek's breakthrough.

** China's blue-chip CSI300 index was up 1.26% at the close, and the Shanghai Composite index rose 1.27% to 3,270.66 points, a five-week high.

** The Hang Seng Index in Hong Kong closed up 1.43% at 20,891.62, a three-month high.

** AI-related sectors led gains in both onshore and offshore markets. The CSI Semiconductor Industry Index (.CSI931865) surged 5.08%, and the Hang Seng Tech Index climbed 2.62% to the highest level since last October.

** Shares of chipmaker Naura surged 8.8%, their biggest single-day advance in nearly three months, while SMIC's Hong Kong-listed shares rallied nearly 7.16% to a record high.

** Enthusiasm for domestic AI-related firms showed no signs of abating after DeepSeek disrupted the global industry last month with advanced models at low cost.

** "DeepSeek's breakthrough is driving a renewed confidence in China's AI sector and it will continue to be a key market theme," analysts at China Securities said in a note.

** It is challenging the prevailing notion that China lags several years behind U.S. competitors in the AI race, and the sector could chart its own course in a revaluation despite global volatility, they added.

** Around the region, MSCI's Asia ex-Japan stock index (.MIAPJ0000PUS) was firmer by 0.44% while Japan's Nikkei index was up 0.61%.

data.title: Eurozone Construction Slump Eases in January, PMI Data Shows
data.description:
The euro area's construction sector remained in contraction territory in January, although the rate of decline in total activity was the slowest in nearly two years.

The HCOB Eurozone Construction PMI Total Activity Index rose to 45.4 from 42.9, Hamburg Commercial Bank and S&P Global said Thursday. The reading indicates the softest contraction since February 2023.

data.title: Compass Group's Growth Prospects Look Good — Market Talk
data.description: Compass Group has a high-quality earnings stream and growth prospects despite an uncertain environment, Shore Capital analyst Greg Johnson writes in a note. The London-listed catering contractor's first-quarter organic sales growth was slightly above forecast and ahead of full-year assumptions, Johnson says. Pricing and volumes will likely ease through the year, with like-for-like volumes a potential positive driver to forecasts, he adds. Shares are down 1.9% at 2,737 pence.(najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

data.title: Finnish Trade Deficit Narrows in December
data.description:
Finland’s trade deficit narrowed to EUR 0.11 billion in December 2024 from EUR 0.52 billion from the corresponding month of the previous year, preliminary estimates showed.

Imports dropped by 1.6% year-on-year to EUR 6.13 billion, due to reduced purchases from non-EU (-9.7%) although it improved for EU countries (5.5%).

Imports fell mostly from United Kingdom (-37.1%), China (-25.5%), Switzerland (-14.0%), and Sweden (-8.5%).

Meanwhile, exports increased by 5.5% to EUR 6.02 billion, boosted by sales to EU (7.2%) and non-EU countries (3.1%).

Among trading partners, exports improved mainly to Latvia (207.7%), US (36.0%), Sweden (27.6%), and Canada (24.5%).

For the whole year of 2024, the country recorded a trade deficit of EUR 2.12 billion.

data.title: Dai Nippon Toryo Co Ltd - To Launch Tender Offer For Shinto Paint At 90 Yen Per Share
data.description:
Dai Nippon Toryo Co Ltd :

  • DAI NIPPON TORYO CO LTD - TO LAUNCH TENDER OFFER FOR SHINTO PAINT AT 90 YEN PER SHARE

  • DAI NIPPON TORYO CO LTD - TO SPEND TOTAL 1.397 BILLION YEN FOR TENDER OFFER FOR SHINTO PAINT

data.title: Uno Minda Gets Board Nod to Raise Up To INR5 Billion Through Debenture Issue
data.description:
Auto components manufacturer Uno Minda has received board approval to raise up to 5 billion Indian rupees through the issue of non-convertible debentures in one or more debentures, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The funds will be used to meet the fund requirement for capital expenditure and investments in subsidiaries, joint ventures, and associate companies of the Uno Minda.

data.title: Booz Allen Hamilton Says Cisco Completes Acquisition of SnapAttack
data.description:
Booz Allen Hamilton said late Wednesday that Cisco has completed its acquisition of SnapAttack, the cyber-security firm that was spun out of Booz Allen in 2021 and in which it continued to retain an investment.

Financial terms were not disclosed.

SnapAttack was incubated and launched publicly by Booz Allen Hamilton's DarkLabs cyber division, the company said.

data.title: GlobalData Kicks Off GBP50 Million Share Repurchase Program
data.description:
GlobalData launched a share buyback program to repurchase 50 million pounds sterling of shares to return capital to shareholders.

The data, analytics, insights, and technology company tapped Investec Bank to buy back up to 84,502,770 shares until Aug. 31, according to a Thursday release.

It noted that all repurchased shares would be canceled.

data.title: Shanghai Henlius Biotech's Anti-Lung Cancer Injection Gets European Commission's Approval
data.description:
Shanghai Henlius Biotech's serplulimab injection in combination with carboplatin and etoposide has been approved by the European Commission for the first-line treatment of adult patients with extensive-stage small cell lung cancer, a Wednesday bourse filing said.

The injection is an anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody used for the treatment of various types of lung cancer. It has been approved in mainland China to be used in combination with chemotherapy.

The injection will be deployed in Europe under the trade name Hetronifly. The approval implies a centralized marketing authorization for the injection for all the EU member states together with Iceland, Liechtenstein, and Norway.

data.title: Another scandal at Nomura as former employee arrested for alleged fraud
data.description:
Japanese financial giant Nomura Holdings said on Thursday that a former employee at its brokerage unit had been arrested on suspicion of fraud, the latest in a string of scandals that have dogged the firm over the past year.

Yuta Cho, 30, who left Nomura in June 2024, has been arrested on fraud charges, Nomura's joint chief compliance officer, Shinichi Mizuno, told a briefing.

The crimes were alleged to have occurred in January 2024, Nomura said in a statement.

Nomura was made aware of the alleged fraud from a customer after Cho had left the company, at which point it immediately spoke to the police, Nomura's vice president and chief of staff, Toshiyasu Iiyama, told the same briefing.

Kyodo News reported earlier that the suspect is accused of having stolen 10 million yen ($65,700) from a woman in her 70s, telling her it would be invested in an interest-bearing savings fund.

Mizuno and Iiyama did not confirm the nature of the charges due to the ongoing police investigation, but said that no other customers had been affected by the alleged fraud. Cho could not be reached for comment.

Another former Nomura employee was charged with crimes including attempted murder and robbery in November, prompting a public apology from the company's head.

And in late October, Japan's banking regulator slapped Nomura with a 21.8 million yen penalty after an investigation determined that a trader manipulated the price of 10-year bond government futures contracts in March 2021.

($1 = 152.2300 yen)

data.title: Czech Republic Inflation Falls to 2.8% in January
data.description:
The annual inflation rate in the Czech Republic fell to 2.8% in January 2025, down from 3% in the previous month, though it was above the expected 2.6%, according to preliminary estimates.

Prices declined for energy goods (-2.4% vs. +2.4% in December), while inflation slowed for services (4.7% vs. 5%) and remained unchanged for goods (1.7%).

On a monthly basis, consumer prices increased by 1.3%, the largest rise since January, exceeding forecasts of 1.1% and reversing a 0.3% decline in December.

data.title: Ram Ratna Wires Extends INR75 Million Unsecured Loan to JV Epavo Electricals
data.description:
Ram Ratna Wires has entered into a loan agreement with joint venture company Epavo Electricals on Feb. 6, to provide an unsecured inter-corporate loan, according to a same-day Indian bourse filing.

The total agreement size is 150 million Indian rupees, with 75 million rupees granted and disbursed so far.

The loan will be used for Epavo's business activities, including capital and operational expenses, Ram Ratna Wires said.

data.title: Boji Medical Technology Receives Patent For Its Tranexamic Acid Drug
data.description:
Boji Medical Technology said it received a new invention patent for its tranexamic acid drug from China's National Intellectual Property Administration, according to a Thursday filing with the Shenzhen Stock Exchange.

The innovation improves drug release control, reduces gastrointestinal irritation, and enhances tablet production efficiency. The patent (No. ZL 2023 1 1771565.5), granted on Jan. 28, covers the drug's composition and manufacturing process.

data.title: Japanese Megabank Executive Calls for BOJ to Hike Rate Above 1% — Market Talk
data.description: The Bank of Japan should raise interest rates to at least above 1% if economic growth continues on greater flexibility in the job market and productivity improvements, says Masamichi Koike, deputy president and executive officer at Japanese megabank Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corp. However, given that the country still hasn't completely overcome a deflationary mindset, "there may be a shock [to the economy] if the BOJ raises rates too rapidly," he says at an event in Tokyo. Meanwhile, Japan's corporate sector will likely witness more consolidations and bankruptcies this year as the economic recovery spurs healthy competition, he says. (megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0835 GMT - The Bank of Japan should raise interest rates to at least above 1% if economic growth continues on greater flexibility in the job market and productivity improvements, says Masamichi Koike, deputy president and executive officer at Japanese megabank Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corp. However, given that the country still hasn't completely overcome a deflationary mindset, "there may be a shock [to the economy] if the BOJ raises rates too rapidly," he says at an event in Tokyo. Meanwhile, Japan's corporate sector will likely witness more consolidations and bankruptcies this year as the economic recovery spurs healthy competition, he says. (megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com)

0802 GMT - Sterling falls as investors exercise caution ahead of the Bank of England's interest-rate decision at 1200 GMT. The market is pricing in a 94% chance of the BOE cutting rates by 25 basis points, according to LSEG. This marks the first decision since the U.K. government bond selloff last month. "Indeed, at the height of the pound's slump, there had been speculation about whether the BOE would cut at all at this meeting," Deutsche Bank analysts say in a note. However, the latest inflation data were lower than expected and this supported market bets for another cut. Deutsche Bank expects a 8-1 vote in favor of a cut. GBP/USD falls 0.2% to 1.2476. EUR/GBP rises 0.1% to 0.8325. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0753 GMT - The dollar edges higher as uncertainty around President Trump's trade policy remains elevated. Safe-haven demand should persist for the time being, with market participants seeking shelter in the dollar as a result of ongoing policy uncertainties, Pepperstone strategist Michael Brown says in a note. Trump's tariff plans will have a less detrimental impact on the U.S. economy compared to developed market peers, he says. This effectively puts the dollar in a position of being "the best of a bad bunch." The DXY dollar index rises 0.2% to 107.768 after hitting a one-week low of 107.296 Wednesday. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0722 GMT - The FTSE 100 is expected to open 73 points higher, or 0.9%, according to IG. The index closed 52 points higher, or 0.6%, to 8523 points Wednesday. Market sentiment across Europe and the U.S. has improved on waning trade tensions with President Trump, Swissquote Bank analyst Ipek Ozkardeskaya says in a note. Investors are also looking ahead to the latest policy decision from the Bank of England at 1200 GMT. The BOE is expected to lower interest rates by 25 basis points as U.K. Treasury chief Rachel Reeves's growth plans are "clouded by the country's restricted finances and the economy calls for BOE support," she says.(renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0714 GMT - The Philippine central bank is likely to cut its benchmark overnight reverse-repurchase rate by 25 bps to 5.50% next week, says HSBC. December and January CPI readings surprised on the upside but remains well within the lower-bound range of the BSP's 2%-4% target band, economist Aris Dacanay writes in a note. The central bank loosening its monetary policy will lend some much needed support to the economy after disappointing 4Q economic growth, he says. HSBC continues to expect the BSP to cut its policy rate by a total of 75 bps this year to 5.00%, while moving back its forecasts for the ending of the cycle to 4Q from 3Q. HSBC also projects the policy rate will remain constant at 5.00% throughout 2026. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0713 GMT - German Bunds may struggle initially as markets digest heavy bond issuance from eurozone peers Spain and France, say Commerzbank Research's Hauke Siemssen and Erik Liem. However, the Bank of England's anticipated interest-rate cut and below-consensus U.S. ISM data should keep the overall bond-positive sentiment intact, the rates strategists say. "Overall, we expect today's duration heavy supply to weigh on markets in the morning but relief should be in store once the auctions have been absorbed," they say. France will auction 11 billion euros-13 billion euros in long-dated bonds, while Spain will sell 5.75 billion euros-7.25 billion euros in bonds across short- to ultra-long maturities, as well as in an inflation-linked bond. The 10-year Bund rises 1.5 basis points to 2.373%, according to LSEG data. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0713 GMT - The risk of a one-off rate cut by the Bank of Thailand after 1Q is increasing given the low inflation and headwinds to growth, says Aris Dacanay, economist at HSBC Global Research. The government aims to lower electricity tariffs this year, he says in a note. Also, with trade tariffs being raised between the U.S. and China, Chinese exports may be rerouted to Thailand, increasing competition among manufacturers and putting a ceiling on their ability to raise prices. This is also a headwind to Thai GDP if consumers substitute local goods for goods produced in China, he says. While a rate cut in 2025 is not his baseline scenario, Dacanay says the headwinds will likely keep the BOT on its toes in the quarters ahead. (monica.gupta@wsj.com)

0703 GMT - Former Bank of Japan Gov. Haruhiko Kuroda endorses the central bank's interest-rate increases and other policy changes made since March 2024, which reverse most of his aggressive monetary easing programs. "It is only natural that the BOJ is normalizing monetary policy," given the economic recovery, price rises and wage growth, Kuroda says at an event in Tokyo. Kuroda plays down criticism among some economists that the BOJ's rate increases are ruining the virtuous cycle of income, spending and inflation. Wages don't have to keep increasing at 5% like last year, the former governor says, adding that the BOJ could achieve sustainable, stable 2% inflation as long as wages grow by around 3%. (megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com)

0655 GMT - The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield has only a little more room to fall, ING's rates strategists say in a note. The strategists have been tactically bullish--or positive in the short term--on U.S. Treasurys in the past couple of weeks, so the recent fall in yields is moving in their short-term direction, they say. "Another 10-20 basis points and it will begin to look like its gone too far, and that's where the backup starts," they say. The 10-year Bund yield rises 1 basis point to 4.432%, according to Tradeweb. The 10-year Treasury yield fell 9 basis points on Wednesday, helped by weaker-than-expected ISM survey data. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0642 GMT - Philippines' hotter-than-expected January inflation print is unlikely to dissuade the central bank from cutting the policy rate by 25 bps next week, but an April rate cut could be at risk if inflation remains stubbornly high in February or March, BofA Global Research says. The monthly rates of price growth have been above average since November 2024, partly due to an unusual spate of bad weather and natural calamities that affected food supplies, says research analyst Jojo Gonzales. Food inflation accelerated to 3.8% on year and 1.1% on month in January from 3.4% on year and 0.8% on month in December, he notes, adding that rice prices haven't eased despite lower import tariffs. (monica.gupta@wsj.com)

0523 GMT - Australia's economic activity has cooled, but larger states such as Victoria have witnessed a sharper slowdown, while smaller states like Western Australia have been more resilient, economists at HSBC Global Research say. With fiscal challenges, pandemic policy and postpandemic behavioral changes affecting migration patterns and housing markets, smaller states will likely continue to outperform in 2025, they say. Affordability in smaller states has attracted people, companies and public agencies allowing more flexible working arrangements, they note. While Victoria's public debt has risen sharply, making it one of the most indebted state governments in the world outside the U.S., Western Australia has benefited from strong activity, high commodity prices, and favorable GST arrangements, they add. (monica.gupta@wsj.com)

0505 GMT - The suspension of a U.S. trade provision allowing goods valued under $800 to enter the country duty-free will likely have an immediate negative impact on Chinese exporters, Nomura analysts say in a research note. Without the de minimis exemption, direct-to-consumer exporters would need to obtain approvals from various regulatory agencies and be subject to higher tariffs, the analysts say. Nomura estimates that 10% of Chinese shipments would no longer be able to enter the U.S. as a result. The analysts expect consumer demand to roughly halve, correlating with an expected 55% price hike. Meanwhile, they estimate that Chinese goods entering the U.S. under the de minimis exemption could plummet 60%. Therefore, ending the de minimis provision could shave off 1.3 percentage points from China's export growth and 0.2 percentage point from GDP growth. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: Correction: Mentice to Present the Company's Year-End Q4 Report 2024 via Webcast
data.description:
Updated Time Webcast Conference Call

Mentice AB (STO: MNTC) announces that the company will present its Year-End Q4-2024 interim report for the period October – December via a webcast conference call on February 13, 2025, at 11:00 CET.

During the webcast, CEO Frans Venker and CFO Ulrika Drotz will provide insights into the company's financial performance and strategic developments. The presentation will be followed by a Q&A session, moderated by Carnegie analyst Rikard Engberg.

The presentation will be held in English, and participants are encouraged to register in advance.

For more information on how to join the webcast and participate, please visit https://investor.mentice.com/.

data.title: AstraZeneca Expects Strong Growth Momentum to Continue in 'Catalyst-Rich' 2025
data.description:
AstraZeneca expects to continue its strong growth momentum in 2025, touting a "catalyst-rich" year marked by late-stage readouts for new medicines and potential indication expansions for existing drugs.

The Anglo-Swedish pharmaceutical giant is set to release the first phase 3 data for seven new molecular entities in 2025, including cancer drugs camizestrant and ceralasertib, according to a Thursday release. Late-stage clinical results will also be available for hypertension medicine baxdrostat, and rare disease therapies anselamimab, efzimfotase alfa, eneboparatide and gefurulimab.

For additional indications, AstraZeneca will announce late-stage readouts for Datroway in lung and breast cancer, Enhertu in HER2+ breast cancer, and Imfinzi in bladder cancer. Outside of oncology, the company is due to provide phase 3 results for Breztri and Fasenra in severe asthma and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, respectively.

"This year marks the beginning of an unprecedented, catalyst-rich period for our company, an important step on our Ambition 2030 journey to deliver $80 billion Total Revenue by the end of the decade," AstraZeneca Chief Executive Officer Pascal Soriot said.

On the financial side, the company forecasts a high single-digit percentage increase in total revenue for 2025, with core EPS growth expected at a low double-digit percentage. Both guidance are at constant exchange rates.

In 2024, the drugmaker's attributable profit reached $7.04 billion, up from $5.96 billion a year ago. Total revenue also climbed to $54.07 billion from $45.81 billion. AstraZeneca said its revenue growth was mainly driven by its oncology segment, followed by respiratory and immunology, and cardiovascular, renal and metabolism therapeutic areas.

data.title: Rayitek Hi-Tech Film Receives 21-Million-Yuan Government Subsidy
data.description:
Rayitek Hi-Tech Film subsidiary Shenzhen Ruihuatai Applied Materials Technology received a government subsidy of 21.2 million yuan, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Shares of the thermal control polyimide and electronic polyimide film producer rose 3% Thursday.
